_comment: This file is OVERWRITTEN each security review. Do NOT append to it.

ssh_attacks.fail2ban_status: active - sshd jail running, 7 currently banned, 3067 total banned
ssh_attacks.change_since_last: Failed attempts at 21015 (up from 20544, +471). fail2ban total bans 3067 (up from 3001, +66). 7 IPs currently banned (up from 5). Continued botnet scanning activity at normal rate. Only authorized logins from novakj via publickey.

pdf_security.innerHTML_note: 35 usages across 11 files: viewer.js(4), upload.js(2), merge.js(4), annotate.js(2), pages.js(4), split.js(6), redact.js(3), img2pdf.js(3), text-overlay.js(2), bookmarks.js(4), ocr.js(1) - all static templates, clearing operations, static SVGs, HTML entities, escaped user data (escapeHtml/escapeAttr), or integer-only interpolation. No user data injected unsafely. search.js, convert.js, compress.js, and watermark.js have zero innerHTML.

bookmarks_module_review.innerHTML_note: 4 usages: clearing container (line 93), static SVG arrow (line 148), filter highlight via highlightMatch() with escapeHtml() (line 168), escapeHtml helper return (line 233). Bookmark titles displayed via textContent (line 164) by default. Filter highlight uses escapeHtml() on all text segments before wrapping match in <mark> tag.
bookmarks_module_review.user_data_handling: PDF outline titles from pdf.js getOutline() displayed via textContent. Only when filter is active, titles rendered through highlightMatch() which escapes all parts via escapeHtml(). Navigation uses pdf.js getDestination/getPageIndex APIs - no user-controlled string interpolation.
bookmarks_module_review.dom_construction: All elements created via document.createElement() with className/style. No template literal HTML with user data.

ocr_module_review.innerHTML_note: 1 usage: static SVG icon for extract button reset (line 249). Extracted text displayed in textarea.value, stats via textContent. No user data in HTML.
ocr_module_review.user_data_handling: Extracted text from pdf.js/Tesseract placed in textarea.value (not innerHTML). Stats rendered via textContent. Download filename uses sanitizeFilename() from utils.js. Page range parsed with parseInt and validated against totalPages.

img2pdf_module_review.innerHTML_note: 3 usages: clearing (line 199), template with escapeHtml/escapeAttr for filenames (line 208), escapeHtml helper return (line 561). Data URLs from FileReader.readAsDataURL interpolated into img src — safe as browser-generated base64.
img2pdf_module_review.image_validation: MIME type via ACCEPTED_TYPES Set + file extension regex. Both checked with OR logic (accepts if either matches).
img2pdf_module_review.user_data_handling: Filenames escaped via escapeHtml() and escapeAttr() before template interpolation. Download filename uses sanitizeFilename() from utils.js. Data URLs from FileReader (browser-safe). Page size/orientation/margin from controlled select elements.

text_overlay_module_review.innerHTML_note: 2 usages: clearing layer (line 353), static x character (line 383). User text rendered via textContent (line 376, 522) — never as HTML.
text_overlay_module_review.user_data_handling: User text stored in overlay.text, rendered via textContent on div elements. Editing uses textarea.value (no HTML parsing). Format controls use parseInt with bounds (8-72), parseFloat, and controlled select elements. Text embedded in PDF via pdf-lib drawText() — rendered as PDF text content, not HTML.
text_overlay_module_review.dom_construction: All overlay elements created via document.createElement() with className/style — no template literal HTML with user data.

redact_module_review.redaction_limitation: Draws opaque rectangles but does NOT remove underlying content streams. Hidden data may still be extractable.

jszip_min_js.purpose: ZIP file creation for split and convert module downloads

known_issues: server_tokens set to 'build' - leaks nginx version (nginx/1.26.3 Ubuntu) in Server header. Should be 'off'.
No general *.md nginx block - only CLAUDE.md specifically blocked. Any new .md file added to webroot would be publicly accessible.
UFW firewall is inactive - only SSH and HTTP/HTTPS ports are needed but no firewall enforces this
disable_symlinks off in nginx config - low risk since only novakj can write to webroot
CGI location block exists in nginx but no CGI scripts present - dead config that could be removed
Codex proxy at /codex returns 502 - backend not running. No security risk but dead endpoint.
TASK-016 redaction tool draws opaque rectangles but does NOT strip underlying content streams - redacted text/images may still be extractable from the PDF file. Users should be warned.

img2pdf.js image validation uses OR logic (MIME type OR extension) - slightly permissive but acceptable for client-side processing since images are rendered via browser Image API which rejects non-images.
notes: Full security review completed - 39 checks passed, 0 critical findings

NEW: Reviewed bookmarks.js module (TASK-019): 4 innerHTML usages, all safe. Bookmark titles rendered via textContent by default. Filter highlight uses escapeHtml() on all segments before wrapping in <mark>. Navigation uses pdf.js getDestination/getPageIndex APIs. No eval/Function/document.write. Fully secure.
Reviewed ocr.js module: 1 innerHTML usage (static SVG reset). Extracted text displayed in textarea.value, stats via textContent. Page range validated. Download uses sanitizeFilename(). Fully secure.
All 21 JS files scanned: no eval(), Function(), document.write(), or unsafe patterns found
35 innerHTML usages across 11 files - all verified safe (up from 27/9; bookmarks.js +4, ocr.js +1, plus 3 previously uncounted in viewer.js/split.js/pages.js)
SSH: Continued botnet scanning. Failed attempts at 21015 (+471). fail2ban total bans 3067 (+66). 7 IPs currently banned (up from 5). Only authorized logins from novakj via publickey.
SSL certificate valid until June 18, 2026 (78 days remaining)
recommendations: MEDIUM: Add user-facing warning in redaction UI that underlying PDF data may not be fully removed - only visually covered.
LOW: Set server_tokens off in nginx.conf to stop leaking version info (currently exposes nginx/1.26.3)
LOW: Enable UFW firewall (sudo ufw allow 22,80,443/tcp && sudo ufw enable)
LOW: Add general *.md deny rule to nginx with explicit exception for known safe files
LOW: Remove unused CGI location block from nginx config (no scripts exist)
LOW: Consider disable_symlinks on in nginx for defense in depth
